journal: Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association
publisher: Oxford University Press for the American Medical Informatics Association
scope: AMIA's biomedical and health informatics journal covering clinical care, clinical research, translational science, implementation science, imaging, education, consumer health, public health, and policy. Strong submissions describe innovative informatics research or systems that advance biomedical science or promote health.

formatting.abstract_style: Structured abstract for Research and Applications: Objective, Materials and Methods, Results, Discussion, Conclusion
formatting.figure_table_policy: For Research and Applications: up to 4 tables and up to 6 figures; images uploaded separately with legends and alt text.
special_requirements: Title page with corresponding author details, author affiliations, up to five keywords, and word count.
Disclose AI tool use where applicable.
Submit related published materials where overlap assessment is needed.

indexing.notes: Indexing should be re-verified through NLM Catalog, Scopus, and Web of Science before displaying production badges.

fit_strengths: Strong fit for biomedical and health informatics work with clear innovation and evaluation.
Strong fit for clinical care, clinical research, translational bioinformatics, implementation science, public health, and policy manuscripts.
Strong fit when the manuscript has implications for biomedical science, health systems, or health outcomes.
fit_risks: Risk increases if the manuscript is not clearly informatics-centered.
Risk increases if evaluation is weak or the system innovation is unclear.
Risk increases if word count, structured abstract, figure/table limits, or disclosure requirements are not met.

checklist: Use JAMIA structured abstract headings for Research and Applications.
Keep Research and Applications manuscripts up to 4000 words and abstract up to 250 words.
Include Background and Significance in the main text.
Use no more than 4 tables and 6 figures for Research and Applications.
Add conflicts, funding, CRediT roles, and AI-use disclosure where applicable.
